content.assessment: This proposal aligns closely with the lens's central methodological demand: before attributing system change to any policy, the underlying measurement must be harmonized and decomposed. The lens's key limitation — that Portugal's own gains cannot be pinned to one intervention — is precisely the trap this proposal seeks to avoid by building decomposition capacity to separate diagnostic-practice, access, and incentive effects from true prevalence, directly responding to the unreconciled multi-source series problem (F-live-portuguese_reform-01) and to contested interpretive claims about awareness-driven identification rise (F-live-portuguese_reform-10).
opportunities: Could serve as the evidentiary backbone enabling future evaluation of any of the other proposals' incremental contributions, directly satisfying the lens's causal-attribution criterion
Provides infrastructure to test whether inter-county variation patterns resembling the 2003 amendment episode (F-live-portuguese_reform-02) recur under current rules
strengths: Directly targets the attribution problem the lens identifies as the primary obstacle to evaluating any SEN policy bundle
Uses documented natural policy variation (rule changes) as identification points, a methodologically appropriate way to approximate the lens's demand for disentangling bundled causal contributions

Would clarify whether the fivefold subgroup increase pattern seen after the 2003 category amendment (F-live-portuguese_reform-02) reflects genuine prevalence change or definitional/administrative artifacts, informing future policy design
threats: Political resistance to publishing incentive-driven over-identification findings could stall the very transparency the lens depends on for causal clarity
weaknesses: A measurement architecture alone does not constitute a policy bundle or quality-levelling and equity intervention; the lens would note that data reconciliation, however necessary, cannot itself be a substitute for coordinated structural reform

Risk that decomposition findings remain interpretively contested, as the analogous awareness/diagnostic-practice explanation in the record is explicitly flagged as weak, qualitative, and non-causal (F-live-portuguese_reform-10)
